login.title: Welcome back.
login.subtitle: Sign in to pick up where you left off.
login.providerMockedBody: No real OAuth is wired in this build.

suspended.title: Account suspended
suspended.body: Your account has been suspended by an administrator. Contact support if you think this is a mistake.

oauthErrors.invalid_or_expired_state: Your sign-in link expired. Please try again.
oauthErrors.state_mismatch: Couldn't verify the sign-in request. Please try again.
oauthErrors.missing_code_or_state: The provider didn't return a valid response.
oauthErrors.token_exchange_failed: Couldn't complete sign-in with the provider.
oauthErrors.profile_fetch_failed: Couldn't read your profile from the provider.
oauthErrors.provider_unavailable: This sign-in method is currently unavailable.
oauthErrors.session_error: Couldn't start your session. Please try again.

register.title: Create your account.
register.subtitle: Free to start. No card required.
register.name: Name
register.captchaWrong: Not aligned — try the new puzzle.
register.ipLimited: Too many accounts created from your network today. Please try again later.
register.verifySubtitle: We sent a 6-digit code to {{email}}. Enter it below.
register.codeSentBody: Check your inbox for a new code.

forgot.title: Forgot password?
forgot.subtitle: Enter your email and we'll send a reset code.
forgot.checkInboxBody: If {{email}} matches an account, we sent a 6-digit code. It expires in 10 minutes.
forgot.resetSubtitle: Enter the code we sent to {{email}} and choose a new password.
forgot.resetSuccessBody: You can now sign in with your new password.

errorCodes.password_too_short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
errorCodes.already_initialized: This deployment is already set up.
errorCodes.setup_required: This deployment needs first-run setup before anyone can register.
errorCodes.email_domain_not_allowed: This email domain isn't allowed to register.
errorCodes.invalid_or_expired_code: That code is invalid or has expired.
errorCodes.invalid_verification_request: This verification request is no longer valid.
errorCodes.account_not_found: No account found for that email.
errorCodes.email_not_verified: Please verify your email before signing in.
errorCodes.twofa_start_failed: Couldn't start two-factor verification. Please try again.
errorCodes.twofa_session_expired: Your sign-in session expired. Please sign in again.
errorCodes.twofa_invalid_session: This sign-in session is no longer valid.
errorCodes.twofa_invalid_code: That code didn't work — try again.
errorCodes.rate_limited: Too many attempts. Please wait a moment and try again.
errorCodes.email_cooldown: An email was sent recently. Please wait before requesting another one.
errorCodes.register_ip_limit: Too many accounts created from your network today. Please try again later.

twofa.title: Two-factor verification
twofa.subtitle: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to finish signing in.
twofa.invalid: That code didn’t work — try again

setup.title: Create your admin account
setup.subtitle: This is the first account on this Aivory instance — it becomes the administrator.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: This policy explains how information is handled when you use this Aivory instance, including conversations, files, AI providers, workspaces, diagnostic records, and the controls available to you.
privacy.notice: Aivory is open-source, self-hosted software. The person or organization that operates this instance decides where it runs, which providers it connects to, and how long data is retained. In this policy, that party is called the instance operator. The Aivory project and its contributors do not automatically receive data from independently operated instances.

sections.heading: 1. Scope and responsible operator
paragraphs: This policy applies to the website, application, APIs, and related services made available through this Aivory instance. It does not replace any supplemental privacy notice supplied by the instance operator, your organization, or a connected third-party service.

The instance operator is responsible for the deployment, user administration, model channels, storage, logs, security configuration, and legal basis applicable to this instance. Contact the operator if you need its identity, address, privacy contact, applicable jurisdiction, or deployment-specific retention schedule.

sections.heading: 2. Account and authentication data
paragraphs: When you create or use an account, the service may store your email address, display name, password hash, account status, user group and permissions, preferences, last activity time, and permanent or time-limited credits. If enabled, it may also store two-factor authentication configuration and links to OAuth or OIDC identities.

Login and refresh sessions may include an IP address, user-agent, timestamps, expiration and revocation state, and an approximate location when supplied by the operator's reverse proxy or infrastructure. Passwords are stored as hashes rather than readable passwords.
items: Authentication uses HttpOnly access and refresh cookies with SameSite protections; the Secure attribute depends on whether the instance is served over HTTPS.
The browser may retain language, theme, editor, workspace, dismissed-announcement, icon-cache, and recovery preferences in local or session storage.
The core project does not include advertising trackers, but an operator may add its own proxy, analytics, monitoring, or logging services.

sections.heading: 3. Cookies, local storage, and device data
paragraphs: Aivory uses browser storage to keep you signed in, complete OAuth handoffs, remember your interface choices, recover certain in-progress actions, and avoid repeatedly downloading unchanged resources. Authentication cookies are necessary for account access; the core application does not use them to build advertising profiles.

Local and session storage can remain on a device after you close a tab. The application, browser, operating system, reverse proxy, or content-delivery layer may also cache static resources, model icons, previews, or downloaded files. Clearing cookies or site data can sign you out and remove local preferences or unsent recovery state, but does not delete information already stored by the instance.
items: Essential cookies can carry short-lived access state, refresh-session state, and one-time OAuth or cross-domain handoff information.
Local preferences can include language, theme, accent, editor behavior, selected workspace, dismissed announcements, application-update state, and pending conversation information.
Network and device records can include IP address, user-agent, request time, referring page, response status, and proxy-supplied location or security signals.

sections.heading: 4. Content you create or provide
paragraphs: The service stores content needed to provide persistent AI workflows. This can include prompts, model responses, conversation branches and titles, citations, reasoning or tool-call blocks, feedback, summaries, message status, and generated artifacts.

It may also store uploaded files, project instructions, knowledge bases, parsed documents, chunks and retrieval metadata, vector embeddings, memories and their source-message links, workspace content, and public-share snapshots. Do not submit information you are not authorized to process or disclose.

sections.heading: 5. Memories and personalization
paragraphs: Long-term memory is configurable and is disabled for new accounts by default. If both the instance and user settings enable it, a background task model may analyze recent user messages from private conversations and extract preferences, facts, relationships, or other reusable context. A memory can include a value, category, confidence, status, and a link to its source message, and active memories may be inserted into later private conversations.

Memory extraction is probabilistic and may be incomplete or wrong. Personal memories are not intended to be extracted from or injected into shared-workspace conversations, but content explicitly placed in a workspace remains visible to its members. You can review, add, edit, or delete memories where those controls are available. Disabling memory can stop future extraction or use; delete existing memory records separately if you also want them removed.
sections.note: Do not rely on memory as an authoritative record. Correct or remove inaccurate, outdated, or unexpectedly sensitive entries.

sections.heading: 6. Usage, billing, and diagnostic records
paragraphs: To enforce limits, calculate credits, troubleshoot failures, and provide administrator reports, the service records operational metadata such as user, conversation and message identifiers; model, provider and channel; request purpose; token, cache and image counts; estimated cost, currency and credits; generation time; fallback use; and success or error status.

Failed provider requests may retain a sanitized copy of the request method, URL, headers, body, and error. The operator can also enable full sanitized request logging or logging for successful requests. Because provider request bodies can contain prompts and conversation context, these settings may cause sensitive content to persist in diagnostic records.
sections.note: Sanitization is intended to mask recognized credentials, but it is not a substitute for avoiding secrets, access tokens, private keys, or highly sensitive personal data in prompts and files.

sections.heading: 7. Why information is used
paragraphs: The instance operator and the software process information only as needed to deliver and administer the configured service.
items: Create accounts, authenticate sessions, apply permissions, and secure access.
Store conversations, projects, files, memories, workspaces, and user preferences.
Route requests to selected models and tools, stream results, create titles and summaries, and retrieve knowledge-base material.
Measure usage, apply quotas and credits, investigate errors, prevent abuse, and maintain reliability.
Provide support, administer shared spaces, comply with legal obligations, and enforce the Terms of Service.

sections.heading: 8. Processing roles and legal bases
paragraphs: The lawful basis and privacy role for a particular deployment depend on who operates it and why it is used. An operator may process information to perform a contract with you, provide a service requested by your organization, pursue legitimate interests such as security and reliability, comply with law, protect vital interests, or act on consent where consent is required. The operator must identify and document the bases that apply in its jurisdiction.

When an employer, school, customer, or other organization directs your use, that organization may be the controller or business responsible for the content, while the instance operator or infrastructure providers act on its instructions. Questions about those roles should be directed to the organization and operator. Where processing relies on consent, withdrawing consent does not affect processing already completed and may make the relevant optional feature unavailable.

sections.heading: 9. AI models and automated processing
paragraphs: Prompts, relevant conversation history, system instructions, attachments, retrieved document excerpts, and tool results may be transmitted to the AI model endpoint selected by you or configured by the operator. Supported channels can include OpenAI-compatible services, Anthropic, Google, and other custom endpoints.

Separate model calls may be used for titles, summaries, memory extraction or review, moderation, verification, embeddings, image generation, routing, or other background tasks. Aivory itself does not contain a separate pipeline that trains foundation models on user conversations.
sections.note: An upstream provider may retain, review, transfer, or use submitted data under its own terms, account settings, and enterprise agreement. Confirm the active provider and its policies with the instance operator before submitting sensitive content.

sections.heading: 10. Files, knowledge bases, OCR, and code execution
paragraphs: Uploaded files can be stored on local disk or configured object storage. Text-capable documents may be parsed locally; scanned PDFs, images, or Office documents without a usable text layer may be sent to a configured OCR service such as MinerU, sometimes through a time-limited object-storage URL. Parsed text, document chunks, embeddings, image descriptions, and retrieval metadata may then be stored for search and knowledge-base use.

When you use Python or other execution tools, submitted code and eligible non-image files may be copied into an isolated sandbox. Outputs, logs, session workspaces, and archives may persist across turns or be stored locally or in object storage until cleaned up. Image uploads are intended for supported vision-model APIs, not for placement in the Python sandbox.
items: OCR source-object cleanup and sandbox cleanup are best-effort operations and may not be immediate.
Generated files can contain content derived from your prompt, source files, tool output, or model output.
Administrators may preview, export, or clear sandbox and stored files for support, security, and service administration.

sections.heading: 11. Images, previews, and generated artifacts
paragraphs: When a selected model supports vision, uploaded images and relevant conversation context can be transmitted through that model's native multimodal API. If the model does not support vision, the service is designed to block new image use and remove historical image parts from the provider request. Removing an image from a model request does not delete its attachment record, original stored object, earlier provider copy, or appearance in conversation history.

Image-generation tools may send prompts and optional reference images to a configured image provider. Returned images, charts, documents, code archives, HTML, and other generated artifacts can be persisted with ownership, MIME type, size, storage path, and conversation links. File previews may render content in the browser; opening active or untrusted output can still carry security and privacy risks.
items: Review images and source files for hidden, identifying, confidential, or copyrighted information before submission.
Download or export important artifacts before their conversation, workspace, account, storage object, or sandbox is deleted.
A preview is a convenience view and does not certify that a file is safe, complete, or identical in every external application.

sections.heading: 12. Voice, search, and external websites
paragraphs: If you use voice input, microphone audio may be streamed through the instance backend to an operator-configured transcription provider. The resulting transcript can be placed in the editor or stored in a sent message. Raw audio is not normally retained as ordinary conversation content, but the provider may apply its own retention rules and operator-enabled ASR debugging can record diagnostic frames or transcript fragments.

Web search and fetch tools send queries or target URLs to configured search services and external websites. Those services and sites may receive the query, requested URL, server IP address, and normal network metadata. Content returned from the web may be stored in conversation or tool records.

sections.heading: 13. Other processors and infrastructure
paragraphs: Depending on how this instance is configured, information may be processed by model, embedding, image-generation, search, web-fetch, transcription, OCR, email, OAuth/OIDC, database, Redis, vector-database, sandbox, monitoring, and object-storage providers. Some components may run on infrastructure controlled by the operator; others may be independent third parties.

The operator chooses these services and is responsible for assessing their terms, security, locations, and data-processing practices. This policy cannot describe provider-specific retention or international transfer rules for every deployment.

sections.heading: 14. Workspaces, public links, and administrator access
paragraphs: Workspace members can access content shared within that workspace, including conversations, projects, knowledge bases, and related files, subject to their role. Other members may be able to continue shared conversations. Workspace owners can manage membership, rotate invitation links, and delete the workspace.

When you create a public conversation link, the service stores a frozen snapshot of the active conversation path. Anyone who has the token can view it without signing in and may copy, forward, cache, or preserve the content. Revoking the link prevents future access through Aivory but cannot retrieve copies already made by others.

Instance administrators can access user accounts, conversations, messages, projects, knowledge bases, files, sandbox contents, workspaces, usage and error records, and backups when needed for support, security, abuse handling, legal compliance, or administration. Administrators may copy, export, restrict, or delete that content.

sections.heading: 15. Disclosures and recipients
paragraphs: Information is disclosed when you direct the service to send it to a model or tool, add it to a workspace, create a public link, or otherwise share it. It may also be disclosed to the operator's personnel and contractors, hosting and infrastructure providers, security or support vendors, and professional advisers under access controls or contractual duties appropriate to their role.

The operator may preserve or disclose information when reasonably believed necessary to comply with law or valid legal process, respond to an emergency, investigate fraud or abuse, protect users and the public, establish or defend legal claims, or maintain service integrity. If the instance or its operating organization is reorganized, transferred, or discontinued, relevant data may be transferred to a successor subject to applicable law and notice obligations.
sections.note: The core Aivory project contains no built-in advertising marketplace or data-broker sale workflow. An independent operator's separate business practices, if any, must be disclosed by that operator.

sections.heading: 16. Storage, retention, backups, and deletion
paragraphs: Active data may be stored in the primary database, local or object-storage files, Redis, a vector database, sandbox archives, browser storage, and operator-created backups. There is no single retention period that applies to every Aivory deployment; the operator's settings, capacity, backup rotation, legal duties, and provider contracts determine actual periods.

Deleting content or an account starts removal from active service records. Account deletion immediately disables the account and revokes sessions, then removes conversations, messages, memories, usage records, files, vectors, and storage paths in background batches. Cleanup can be delayed by queues, failures, caches, pending storage cleanup, backup rotation, or copies retained by third parties or recipients.
sections.note: The operator may retain limited records when reasonably required for security, fraud prevention, dispute resolution, legal compliance, or restoration of system integrity.

sections.heading: 17. Your controls and privacy requests
paragraphs: Available controls depend on your permissions and the instance configuration.
items: Edit or delete conversations, messages, files, memories, projects, knowledge bases, and public shares where the interface permits.
Clear conversation history and memories, revoke login sessions, disconnect OAuth identities, leave workspaces, or ask an owner to remove shared content.

Export the currently supported personal archive. The built-in export covers conversations, full message history, memories, and the export timestamp; it is not a copy of every account, file, usage, provider-log, or backup record.
Delete your account from Settings or contact the instance operator to request access, correction, restriction, portability, objection, or deletion rights that apply under local law.

sections.heading: 18. Security and international processing
paragraphs: Aivory includes access controls, password hashing, token revocation, permission checks, request sanitization, and isolated execution mechanisms. Deployment security - including HTTPS, encryption at rest, network controls, backups, monitoring, and administrator practices - is configured by the instance operator. No system can guarantee absolute security.

Configured providers and infrastructure may process data in countries other than yours. The instance operator is responsible for identifying relevant locations and implementing any contracts, safeguards, notices, or consent required for international transfers.

sections.heading: 19. Children, policy changes, and contact
paragraphs: This service is not intended for children who cannot lawfully consent to data processing or enter the Terms of Service in their location. A parent, guardian, school, or organization that enables access for a minor is responsible for obtaining any required authorization and supervising use.

The operator may update this policy when the software, configuration, providers, or legal requirements change. The date above identifies this version. Material changes should be communicated through the instance or another reasonable channel. Direct privacy questions and requests to the administrator or organization that operates this instance.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.intro: These Terms govern access to and use of this Aivory instance, including AI conversations, files, workspaces, public sharing, search, code execution, and other configured tools.
terms.notice: The service is provided by the person or organization operating this Aivory instance, not automatically by the Aivory open-source project or its contributors. Deployment-specific notices, pricing, support commitments, and policies supplied by the operator also apply. If they conflict with these Terms, ask the operator which terms control.

sections.heading: 1. Agreement and service provider
paragraphs: By creating an account, accepting an invitation, or using this instance, you agree to these Terms and the Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, do not use the service. The instance operator is the service provider and is responsible for user administration, infrastructure, connected providers, support, and deployment-specific legal obligations.

Aivory source code is separately licensed under the Apache License 2.0 in the project repository. These Terms govern use of this hosted instance and do not reduce rights granted by that open-source license.

sections.heading: 2. Eligibility and authority
paragraphs: You may use the service only if you can lawfully enter this agreement and are not prohibited from receiving the service. If you use it for an employer, school, team, or other organization, you represent that you are authorized to accept these Terms for that organization and will follow its policies.
A minor may use the service only with the authorization and supervision required by applicable law and the operator's rules.

sections.heading: 3. Accounts and security
paragraphs: Provide accurate registration information, keep credentials and two-factor recovery information secure, and promptly update information that changes. You are responsible for activity performed through your account unless caused by a security failure attributable to the operator.

Do not share accounts, impersonate another person, transfer access without permission, or use someone else's OAuth identity. Notify the operator promptly if you suspect unauthorized access. The operator may revoke sessions, require credential changes, or restrict an account to protect the service.

sections.heading: 4. Service description and configuration
paragraphs: Aivory is a configurable multi-model AI workspace. Features can include chat, projects, memories, knowledge bases, file processing and previews, workspaces, public shares, image generation, voice transcription, web search and fetch, and isolated code execution.

Available models, tools, storage, limits, safety controls, and results vary by operator configuration, user group, provider availability, and software version. A feature shown in the open-source project or documentation may not be enabled on this instance.

sections.heading: 5. Memory and personalization features
paragraphs: If you enable long-term memory, the service may use a task model to extract reusable facts or preferences from your private messages and include active memories in later private conversations. Extracted memories are generated automatically, may be inaccurate, and are not a substitute for records you maintain yourself.

Review memory entries and remove information that is wrong, outdated, or too sensitive. Disabling memory may stop future extraction or use but does not necessarily delete entries that already exist. Personal memory is not intended to be injected into shared-workspace conversations; that separation does not make content you directly share with a workspace private from its members or administrators.

sections.heading: 6. AI output and human review
paragraphs: AI output can be incorrect, incomplete, outdated, biased, offensive, insecure, or similar to material generated for other users. Models may fabricate facts, citations, code behavior, or file contents. You must review output and independently verify information before relying on or sharing it.

The service is not a substitute for qualified medical, legal, financial, employment, educational, safety, or other professional judgment. Do not use output as the sole basis for decisions that can materially affect a person's rights, health, finances, liberty, education, employment, housing, or access to essential services.

sections.heading: 7. Provider selection and sensitive data
paragraphs: A model name in the interface can be connected to a first-party provider, a compatible gateway, a relay, or a custom endpoint selected by the operator. A fallback channel may be used after a main channel fails. Requests can therefore travel through more than one organization or country, and changing models can change the recipient, format, capabilities, cost, and retention rules.

Before entering trade secrets, regulated data, source code, credentials, health or financial records, or other sensitive material, confirm that you and your organization permit that provider and deployment path. Provider safety systems may inspect, reject, truncate, or retain requests independently of Aivory. Never place passwords, API keys, private keys, session cookies, or authentication tokens in a prompt unless a specifically approved workflow requires it.

sections.heading: 8. Your content and processing permission
paragraphs: As between you and the operator, you retain the rights you have in prompts, files, instructions, feedback, and other content you submit. You are responsible for that content and for having all permissions needed to upload, process, share, and instruct the service to use it.

You grant the operator and its configured service providers a limited, non-exclusive permission to host, copy, transmit, parse, transform, embed, retrieve, display, and otherwise process your content only to operate, secure, support, and improve the configured service, comply with law, and enforce these Terms. This permission lasts as long as needed for those purposes and applicable retention periods.

sections.heading: 9. Rights and restrictions in generated output
paragraphs: To the extent permitted by applicable law and the relevant provider terms, you may use output generated for you. These Terms do not promise that output is copyrightable, assign ownership that the operator does not possess, or guarantee that output is exclusive. The same or similar output may be generated for others, and prompts or outputs can contain protected names, styles, code, data, or material supplied by third parties.

You are responsible for determining whether your intended use requires attribution, permission, disclosure, a license, source-code compliance, privacy review, or human authorship. Do not remove third-party notices, imply endorsement, register rights you do not own, or publish output when you know it unlawfully copies, exposes, or misrepresents another person's material.

sections.heading: 10. Files, external content, and execution tools
paragraphs: Files, OCR, web retrieval, generated downloads, previews, and code execution can expose content to third-party services or produce active, malicious, inaccurate, or incompatible output. Inspect generated code and files before running, opening, publishing, or distributing them, and use an appropriate isolated environment.

Do not treat the sandbox as permanent storage or an absolute security boundary. Do not use it to attack systems, mine cryptocurrency, maintain unauthorized services, evade controls, or process data you are not permitted to use. You are responsible for external sites you request the service to access and for complying with their terms and access restrictions.

sections.heading: 11. Workspaces, invitations, and public sharing
paragraphs: Content placed in a workspace can be accessed and continued by other members according to their role. Use invitation links carefully, review membership, and do not place content in a shared space unless every authorized member may view and use it. Workspace owners may remove members, rotate invitations, or delete the workspace.

A public share link can be opened without an account by anyone who obtains its token. You are responsible for reviewing the frozen snapshot and its attachments before sharing. Revocation stops future access through the service but cannot retract copies, screenshots, caches, or onward disclosures made by recipients.

sections.heading: 12. Confidentiality and organizational information
paragraphs: The service is not automatically approved for confidential, classified, privileged, regulated, or export-controlled information. Your employer or organization may impose stricter rules than these Terms. Obtain required authorization and verify the operator's security, provider agreements, data locations, logging settings, access controls, and retention practices before using such information.

Administrators may access content for the purposes described in the Privacy Policy, and workspace members or public-link recipients are not bound to confidentiality merely because content originated in Aivory. Any separate confidentiality agreement between you, your organization, and the operator governs only according to its own terms. Marking a prompt or file as confidential does not change the technical data path.

sections.heading: 13. Acceptable use
paragraphs: You must comply with applicable law, these Terms, operator rules, and the terms of connected providers. You must not use the service to:
items: Commit, facilitate, or conceal illegal, fraudulent, deceptive, threatening, harassing, exploitative, or privacy-invasive conduct.
Create or distribute child sexual abuse material, terrorist content, credible threats of violence, or instructions intended to cause serious physical harm.
Infringe intellectual property, confidentiality, privacy, publicity, database, or other rights, or upload data you lack authority to process.
Develop or deploy malware, credential theft, destructive code, unauthorized surveillance, spam, phishing, or attempts to gain unauthorized access.

Probe, overload, disrupt, scrape, reverse engineer, or bypass authentication, moderation, rate limits, quotas, model restrictions, or other safeguards, except where an open-source license or applicable law expressly permits analysis.
Misrepresent AI output as verified human work where that would deceive others, or make high-impact automated decisions without meaningful human review.
Resell, share, or automate access in a way that violates operator authorization, provider terms, or the technical limits of the instance.

sections.heading: 14. Plans, quotas, credits, and purchases
paragraphs: The operator may assign user groups, storage limits, project or knowledge-base limits, daily message or image quotas, permanent credits, time-limited credits, and redemption codes. Usage calculations can depend on the selected model, token counts, image generation, configured prices, or provider-reported usage.

Any displayed price, credit value, expiration, purchase link, tax, refund right, or payment term is supplied by the operator or an external seller. The core project does not itself process payments. Unless the operator expressly states otherwise or law requires it, credits are personal, non-transferable, not redeemable for cash, and may expire according to the disclosure made when issued.

Redemption codes may add credits, extend the current group, or immediately replace one group with another. Codes can have time, quantity, account, or status restrictions and may be disabled or exhausted. The operator may correct balances or benefits affected by error, fraud, duplicate redemption, chargeback, or abuse, subject to applicable law and any purchase terms disclosed to you.

sections.heading: 15. Third-party services and open-source components
paragraphs: The instance may connect to independent model, search, transcription, OCR, email, identity, storage, database, vector, monitoring, or sandbox services. Their availability, output, security, retention, and use of submitted data are governed by their own terms and agreements with the operator. The operator is not able to guarantee every third-party service.
Aivory and its dependencies include open-source software governed by their respective licenses. Third-party names, models, and trademarks belong to their owners and do not imply endorsement.

sections.heading: 16. Administration, moderation, and enforcement
paragraphs: The operator may use automated checks and administrator review to investigate errors, abuse, security incidents, quota evasion, and suspected violations. Administrators can access conversations, files, knowledge bases, workspaces, sandbox contents, usage logs, and backups as described in the Privacy Policy.

The operator may block a request, remove or restrict content, disable a model or tool, reduce limits, revoke sessions, suspend an account, or preserve relevant records when reasonably necessary to protect users, providers, the service, or the public, or to comply with law.

sections.heading: 17. Availability and service changes
paragraphs: The service may be unavailable because of maintenance, updates, capacity, provider failures, network conditions, security events, or operator decisions. Unless the operator makes a separate written commitment, no uptime, response-time, data-recovery, or support service level is promised.

Models, tools, interfaces, limits, and storage behavior may change or be discontinued. Preview, beta, or experimental features may be less reliable and may change without notice. Keep independent copies of content you cannot afford to lose.

sections.heading: 18. Exports, backups, and risk of data loss
paragraphs: The built-in personal export is a convenience archive of supported conversations, messages, and memories. It is not a complete account, file-system, provider-log, usage-log, vector-database, sandbox, or backup export, and importing it may not restore every branch, attachment, model state, permission, or external artifact.

The operator may create backups and may restore the instance to an earlier state, but no backup schedule, recovery point, or recovery time is guaranteed unless separately agreed in writing. Provider outages, storage failures, software defects, administrative actions, quota changes, or account deletion can cause content or artifacts to become unavailable. Maintain independent, tested copies of important source material and output.

sections.heading: 19. Suspension, termination, and account deletion
paragraphs: You may stop using the service and request account deletion at any time. The operator may suspend or terminate access for violations, security or legal risk, nonpayment where applicable, prolonged inactivity under a disclosed policy, or discontinuation of the instance. Where reasonable, the operator should provide notice and an opportunity to export supported data unless immediate action is necessary.

Account deletion disables access and starts background removal from active systems. Shared content, public copies, backups, diagnostic records, legal holds, and third-party provider records may remain for the periods described in the Privacy Policy.

sections.heading: 20. Service rights, feedback, and infringement
paragraphs: These Terms do not transfer ownership of your content. The operator and relevant licensors retain rights in the hosted service, branding, configuration, documentation, and non-user content, while the Aivory source code remains governed by its repository license.

If you provide feedback, you allow the operator and project maintainers to use it without restriction or compensation, provided it does not identify you or disclose your confidential content. Report claimed infringement or unlawful content to the instance operator with enough detail to locate and assess the material.

sections.heading: 21. Disclaimers, liability, and responsibility
paragraphs: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, the service, models, tools, and output are provided on an 'as is' and 'as available' basis. The operator disclaims implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, accuracy, non-infringement, and uninterrupted or error-free operation where such disclaimers are lawful.

To the maximum extent permitted by law, the operator, Aivory contributors, and their suppliers are not liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, exemplary, or punitive damages, or for lost profits, data, goodwill, or opportunities arising from use of the service. Any liability that cannot be excluded is limited only to the extent allowed by applicable law and any deployment-specific agreement.

You are responsible for your content, instructions, sharing choices, downstream use of output, and violations caused by your conduct. Where permitted by law, you will reimburse the operator for third-party claims and reasonable costs arising from your unlawful content, misuse, or material breach of these Terms, except to the extent caused by the operator.

sections.heading: 22. Changes, local law, and contact
paragraphs: The operator may update these Terms to reflect changes in the service, providers, risks, or law. The date above identifies the current version. Material changes should be communicated through the instance or another reasonable channel; continued use after the effective date means you accept the revised Terms where permitted by law.

Mandatory rights and laws in your jurisdiction continue to apply. No governing law, court, arbitration forum, company address, or support commitment is stated here because those details depend on the operator.
